novel_features.name: Sales velocity board
novel_features.description: Ranks all your live events by tickets sold per day since on-sale and projects a sell-out date.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining synced events, orders, and ticket classes in the local store to compute per-event rate-over-time — no single Eventbrite endpoint, SDK, or MCP server exposes cross-event velocity.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to know which of an organizer's events are underperforming right now, not just current totals.

novel_features.name: Repeat-attendee finder
novel_features.description: Finds fans who bought into two or more of your events, across your whole synced history.
novel_features.rationale: A cross-event SQLite join over the attendee table by normalized email — it restores exactly the cross-event search Eventbrite removed from the public API in 2020.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for loyalty and re-marketing questions an agent cannot answer from any one event's attendee list.

novel_features.name: Discount-code performance
novel_features.description: Per discount code: redemptions, type, and the redemption rate (share of the code's allotment used).
novel_features.rationale: Reads synced discount objects locally — Eventbrite ships no discount-performance report, so ranking codes by redemptions and redemption rate only happens once they live in the store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to measure whether a promo code actually drove sales before renewing or killing it.

novel_features.name: Multi-org client roll-up
novel_features.description: Single pane across every organization your token can see: events count, tickets sold, gross, and top event per org.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates synced orders per organization — every existing Eventbrite MCP server and SDK helper is single-org, so the cross-org agency view does not exist anywhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for an agency or multi-brand operator who needs one client roll-up instead of logging into each org.

novel_features.name: Door roster + check-in gap
novel_features.description: Offline attendee roster for one event with checked-in vs not-yet, VIP and comp flags, door-sorted.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the local attendee store so it works when venue wifi is flaky, and shapes output for will-call — neither the Organizer app nor the API gives a grep-able offline roster.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this at the door when an agent needs a fast who-has-not-checked-in list without a live API round-trip.

novel_features.name: Capacity headroom rollup
novel_features.description: Sold vs total capacity and percent remaining across all your live events at once.
novel_features.rationale: Joins synced events with their ticket classes and inventory tiers — the dashboard shows capacity one event at a time, never the cross-event headroom view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to spot which events are near sell-out or badly under-sold in a single call.

novel_features.name: Refund / cancellation rate
novel_features.description: Refunded and cancelled order counts, refunded revenue, and the rate per event or across the org.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates synced order status across events — the per-event dashboard buries refunds, so the cross-event rate only emerges from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to flag events with abnormal refund rates that signal a pricing, date, or fulfillment problem.

novel_features.name: Top buyers by spend
novel_features.description: Ranks ticket buyers by total spend across all your events, not just by how many events they attended.
novel_features.rationale: Joins synced orders and attendees by normalized email and sums order totals into a lifetime-value view — the dashboard reports spend one event at a time and never aggregates a buyer across events.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find an organizer's highest-value customers for VIP or pre-sale outreach.

novel_features.name: Attendee contact export
novel_features.description: Exports unique attendee contacts (email, name, events attended, check-in status), deduped across all your events and filterable by event.
novel_features.rationale: Dedupes attendees across all synced events into one re-marketing list; the dashboard exports a single event at a time with no cross-event dedupe.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to build a clean, deduped contact list for an email campaign across an organizer's whole audience.

narrative.headline: Every Eventbrite organizer endpoint, plus a local SQLite mirror of your events, orders, and attendees you can search offline — the cross-event search Eventbrite removed, restored over your own data.
narrative.value_prop: Existing Eventbrite tools wrap a handful of event CRUD calls one event at a time. This CLI mirrors the full v3 organizer API and syncs your events, orders, attendees, ticket classes, and discounts into a local SQLite store, then layers cross-event analytics on top: sales-velocity ranks your live events by sell rate, repeat-attendees surfaces returning fans across your whole history, and org-rollup gives agencies a single pane across every client organization.
narrative.auth_narrative: Eventbrite uses an OAuth2 private token sent as a Bearer header. For your own account you do not need the full OAuth dance — copy a private token from eventbrite.com/platform/api-keys and set EVENTBRITE_API_KEY. Run `eventbrite-pp-cli doctor` to confirm it is picked up.

quickstart.comment: Confirm EVENTBRITE_API_KEY is set and the API is reachable before anything else.
Pull everything your token can access into local SQLite; Eventbrite paginates with continuation tokens, which sync follows automatically.
Rank your live events by tickets sold per day so you can see laggards at a glance.
Find fans who attended multiple events — the cross-event view Eventbrite's public search no longer provides.
Full-text search across everything you have synced offline.

troubleshoots.fix: Set EVENTBRITE_API_KEY to a private token from eventbrite.com/platform/api-keys, then run `eventbrite-pp-cli doctor`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Public event search returns nothing or 404
troubleshoots.fix: Eventbrite removed public event search in 2020; sync your org first with `sync` and search the local store with `search` or `repeat-attendees`.

troubleshoots.symptom: sync stops before all records load
troubleshoots.fix: List endpoints page with continuation tokens; re-run `eventbrite-pp-cli sync --full` to walk every page.
These read the local store — run `eventbrite-pp-cli sync` first.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent manages an organizer's Eventbrite presence: creating and publishing events, pulling order and attendee history, and answering cross-event questions (which events sell slowest, which fans return, who the top buyers are, how a discount code performed) that the per-event dashboard and existing single-org MCP servers cannot answer. It is the right tool whenever the task needs the whole order/attendee history in one place rather than a single live API lookup. When the same promoter also sells on DICE, both CLIs emit JSON and a local SQLite store, so an agent can join the two datasets by event (name and date) and by buyer email for a cross-platform performance and loyalty picture.

recipes.title: Find your slowest-selling live events
recipes.explanation: Ranks every live event by tickets sold per day since on-sale with a projected sell-out date, computed from the local order store.

recipes.title: Surface returning fans for re-marketing
recipes.explanation: Cross-event attendee join; --agent emits structured rows and --select narrows to just the contact fields an email tool needs.

recipes.title: Door check-in roster, offline
recipes.explanation: Prints the synced attendee roster for one event with checked-in status as CSV for a will-call sheet — no live API call required.

recipes.title: Agency Friday client roll-up
recipes.explanation: One pane across every organization the token can see: events, tickets sold, gross, and top event per client org.

recipes.title: Measure a promo code's ROI
recipes.explanation: Ranks discount codes by redemptions and redemption rate (share of each code's allotment used) from the synced discount objects.

recipes.title: Combine Eventbrite + DICE for full event performance
recipes.explanation: Export your Eventbrite buyer/attendee list as JSON; a promoter selling the same show on DICE exports the DICE side too, then an agent joins events by name and date and buyers by normalized email for a cross-platform performance and loyalty view no single platform shows.

trigger_phrases: which of my events are selling slowest
check in roster for my event
how did my discount code perform
